preview_text:
The model favors Netherlands at 68% to win, leaving Sweden with an 8% chance of victory. This is a heavily asymmetric matchup according to the statistical forecast, positioning the Netherlands as a strong favorite on a neutral pitch.

The draw sits at 24%, suggesting modest but real likelihood of a stalemate. The 60-percentage-point gap between Netherlands' win probability and Sweden's reflects a one-sided expected outcome, though the 24% draw probability indicates the match is not a foregone conclusion. Competitive balance is low; the model sees Sweden as a substantial underdog.

The market consensus diverges notably from the model. Sportsbooks price Netherlands at 56% and Sweden at 20%—a 12-percentage-point gap favoring the Swedes relative to the model's assessment. Polymarket aligns closely with sportsbooks. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the spread reflects genuine disagreement about Swedish prospects on the day.
